title: romneya coulteri var. trichocalyx
intro: Romneya coulteri var. trichocalyx, also known as the Coulter's marigold or golden poppy, is a perennial plant species within the genus Romneya. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. Its extracts have been reported to possess various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and potentially exhibiting antimicrobial activity. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and salves, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
